subject: [PATCH v5 20/36] mm/memcontrol: account private-node memory in per-node stats

2026-07-20.patch_summary: This patch modifies the Linux kernel's memory control system to account private-node memory in per-node statistics. Currently, private nodes are not included in per-node views of memory usage because their lruvec counters are not refreshed. This patch fixes this issue by including N_MEMORY_PRIVATE in the flush and display process, ensuring that private-node memory is correctly reported per node. The changes affect two files: mm/memcontrol-v1.c and mm/memcontrol.c. The patch is unconditional and does not depend on future capabilities.
